judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are nearly equivalent, covering (a)-(d) with proper citations; neither explicitly flags the draft's mis-ordering (self-congratulatory opening deferring remediation to the end) called for in (e), though Report 2 gestures at the 'lead with impact/remediation' style-guide rule in a different context.

report_1.evidence: failures began 14:03, the first page didn't fire until 14:50 (47 minutes later)... a ~47-minute detection gap

report_2.evidence: failures began at 14:03 but the first alert did not fire until 14:50 (47 minutes later)

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md:9-10` states 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md` states 1,284 transactions failed and required manual reprocessing, and 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv:6` shows April 2026 uptime at 99.77%. `larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md:3-4` defines the contractual monthly SLA as 99.9%, states any month below that is a breach

report_2.evidence: April 2026 uptime was 99.77% (`larkpay-comms/metrics/uptime-2026.csv`), below the contractual 99.9% monthly SLA (`larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md`), which states any month below 99.9% is a breach owing service credits.

report_1.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1 says "Never assign blame to an individual"... This needs to be rewritten to describe the failure mode without blaming a person or naming internal systems.

report_2.evidence: `larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md` rule 1 explicitly says "Never assign blame to an individual,"... remove the "on-call engineer" blame language and the "checkout autoscaler" internal system name, per the style guide.
